channel.title: Crypto Daily Brief
channel.description: Your free daily 5-minute crypto and Web3 news podcast. Bitcoin, Ethereum, DeFi, NFTs, and blockchain news every morning. Stay ahead of the crypto market.
channel.itunes:author: Crypto Daily Brief

item.title: Crypto News Jun 29 — Institutions dumped Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs
item.description: Institutional investors are sending a clear message to the crypto market: it's time to get selective. According to recent data, institutions have been dumping Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs, but they're still buying up XRP and HYPE. This trend shows that institutions are separating their broad crypto risk from targeted altcoin exposure, essentially saying that not all cryptocurrencies are created equal. This matters because it indicates a growing maturity in the market, where investors are no longer ...

item.title: Crypto News Jun 29 — Institutions dumped Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs
item.description: Institutions are sending a clear message to the crypto market, and it's time to decode their strategy. According to recent data, institutions have been dumping Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs at an alarming rate, with outflows dwarfing the inflows of other cryptocurrencies. However, amidst this mass exodus, they're still buying into XRP and HYPE, which suggests that they're not entirely bearish on the crypto market. This selective demand for specific altcoins indicates that institutions are drawing a ...

item.title: Crypto News Jun 30 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Bitcoin is on the move, and today was no exception, with the crypto market seeing a flurry of activity that's got everyone talking. If you're like most of us, you're probably wondering what happened in crypto today, and the truth is, it's been a wild ride. From daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, to the latest developments in blockchain, DeFi, Web3, and crypto regulation, there's been no shortage of news to keep up with. The good news is that despite the volatility, the crypto space...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jun 30 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: A wild ride in the world of crypto today, with markets taking a hit and major players making moves that will have significant implications for the future of this space. To keep you up to speed, let's dive into the latest news and trends that are making waves. The top story today is all about the current state of crypto, with Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3, and crypto regulation all being impacted by daily trends and events. It's essential to stay informed about these developments, as they...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jun 30 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Crypto markets are on high alert today as Bitcoin price volatility reaches a fever pitch, and if you're struggling to keep up with the latest trends and events, you're not alone - the crypto space is moving at lightning speed, with daily trends and events impacting blockchain, DeFi, Web3, and crypto regulation. To get up to speed, let's take a look at the latest news, where we see a mixed bag of developments that are shaping the future of crypto. From Bitcoin price fluctuations to regulatory upd...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 01 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Crypto prices are on a wild ride, with Bitcoin plummeting to new lows and Ethereum struggling to regain its footing, leaving investors wondering if this is the bottom of the market or just the beginning of a long downturn. But that's not all that's happening in the world of crypto today - we've got a slew of new developments that are changing the game for investors and traders. Let's dive into the latest news and trends impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3, and crypto regulation. From...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 02 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Bitcoin just blasted past $60,000, and the entire crypto market is feeling the heat, but what's driving this surge and how will it impact your portfolio? To answer that, let's dive into the latest news and trends shaping the crypto and Web3 world. Today, we saw a significant uptick in crypto prices, with Ether, Solana, and Dogecoin all seeing green after comments from former Fed governor Kevin Warsh pushed Bitcoin above the $60,000 mark. This is a huge deal, as it shows that even the slightest h...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 03 — Crypto Shorts Get Rekt as Bitcoin, Ethereum and XR
item.description: If you're a crypto bear, you might want to look away, because this week has been a wild ride for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP, with all three soaring to their weekly highs. Bitcoin, in particular, has been on a tear, rising to over $62,000 after hitting a 21-month low earlier this week. This sudden surge has left many crypto shorts reeling, and it's a testament to the unpredictable nature of the crypto market. The fact that Bitcoin can bounce back so quickly from a low point is a reminder that thi...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 04 — Tim Draper denies moving Bitcoin, reiterates $250,
item.description: Billionaire investor Tim Draper is back in the headlines, and this time it's not just about his bold predictions for Bitcoin. Yesterday, blockchain analysts linked him to a wallet that transferred a whopping 1,000 BTC to Coinbase Prime, sparking speculation that he was moving his Bitcoin stash. But Draper has come out swinging, denying any involvement in the transfer and reiterating his infamous $250,000 Bitcoin price prediction. This matters because Draper's comments have a significant impact o...

item.title: Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 05 — Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Bitcoin is bouncing back, and the crypto world is buzzing - but what does it all mean for your portfolio? The latest news is in, and it's a must-know for anyone invested in the space. Today's headlines are dominated by the resurgence of Bitcoin, with prices trending upwards and experts weighing in on what this could mean for the market. According to the latest reports, the entire crypto ecosystem is experiencing a significant shift, with daily trends and events impacting everything from Bitcoin ...

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 06
item.description: A whopping $1.9 million in Bitcoin, dormant for nearly 15 years, just moved in a New York lawsuit, sparking widespread curiosity about the owner's identity and the implications for the crypto market. This jaw-dropping development has the entire crypto community talking, and we're diving right into the story. The Bitcoin address in question has been inactive since 2011, and its sudden movement has raised more questions than answers. The lawsuit, which seeks ownership of thousands of inactive Bitc...

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 08
item.description: Bitcoin's dominance has just dropped to a staggering one-month low of 54%, down from 58.12%, according to CoinGecko's dominance table, and this sudden shift is sending shockwaves throughout the crypto market. This means that the "Others" bucket, representing everything outside Bitcoin, Ethereum, and stablecoins, has climbed from 19.39% to 24.68% of total crypto market cap, indicating a significant change in the market dynamics. As a result, altcoin winners are starting to break away, and investo...

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 09
item.description: $127 million in insider tokens are about to flood the market as Pump Fun's July 12 unlock looms, sparking concerns that trader demand may not be enough to absorb the supply without forcing a deeper repricing. This massive token dump is nearly twice PUMP's recent daily volume, and it's got everyone in the crypto community on edge, wondering what this will mean for the token's price. The July 12 unlock is a major test of the market's appetite for PUMP, and if demand can't keep up, we could see a s...

item.title: New Hampshire rejects $100M Bitcoin-backed bond after — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 10
item.description: New Hampshire just rejected a $100 million Bitcoin-backed bond, a decision that could have far-reaching implications for the use of cryptocurrency in government finance. The rejection came after a public finance hearing on July 8, where officials ultimately decided that the risks associated with Bitcoin collateral were too great. This is a significant setback for proponents of Bitcoin-backed bonds, who had hoped that New Hampshire would pave the way for other states to follow suit. The use of Bi...

item.title: U.S. Representatives Urge Senate to Vote on CLARITY Act in — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 11
item.description: One thousand four hundred Bitcoin, worth a staggering $87 million, were just dumped by Empery Digital, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury firm, in a move that has left many in the crypto community wondering what's behind this massive sale. According to reports, the firm has been selling off its Bitcoin holdings since May to fuel an AI data center deal, pay off legal bills, and cover other expenses. This news is significant because it shows that even major players in the crypto space are not immune...

item.title: Bitcoin Slips to $62,000, Paring Rebound as CryptoQuant — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 12
item.description: Bitcoin just slipped to $62,000, paring its recent rebound as CryptoQuant suggests there's still room for the cryptocurrency to move higher. This sudden drop is particularly noteworthy given that Bitcoin had been on a tear, rebounding from last week's $57,700 low. According to CryptoQuant, improving demand and seasonal trends could be the driving forces behind this potential upward movement. This is significant because it indicates that despite the volatility, there are still underlying factors ...

item.title: After MiCA deadline, majority of Binance users sent funds — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 13
item.description: Seventy percent of Binance's European users yanked their funds out of the exchange and into self-custody wallets, rather than moving them to compliant rivals, after the MiCA deadline hit. This is a staggering number, especially considering the regulatory uncertainty that's been plaguing the crypto space. What's even more surprising is that this move suggests European users are more interested in taking control of their own assets, rather than relying on supervised exchanges. This could be a majo...

item.title: Thai scammer's $122M wallet, Japan embraces crypto credit — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 14
item.description: A Thai scammer's wallet just got frozen with a staggering $122 million in crypto, and this case is sending shockwaves through the entire Asian market, with Japan now embracing crypto credit in a big way. This monumental seizure is a wake-up call for the entire crypto community, as it exposes the darker side of the industry, where scammers and fraudsters have been operating with impunity. The fact that this scammer was able to amass such a huge fortune in crypto is a stark reminder of the lack of...

item.title: Bitmine generated $46M from Ethereum staking last quarter — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 15
item.description: Bitmine just raked in $46 million from Ethereum staking last quarter, a staggering 98% of their total revenue, and it's clear that their pivot from Bitcoin mining is paying off in a big way. This is a huge deal because it shows that Ethereum staking is becoming a major player in the crypto space, and companies like Bitmine are positioning themselves to take advantage of this trend. The fact that they launched their validator just a few months ago in March and are already seeing such significant ...

item.title: President Trump To Meet Senators On Clarity Act’s Ethics — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 16
item.description: President Trump is set to meet with senators to discuss the CLARITY Act's ethics provisions, which are currently threatening to derail the bill's chances of passing, and this high-stakes meeting could be the make-or-break moment for the future of crypto regulation in the US. The CLARITY Act has been a major topic of discussion in the crypto world, as it aims to provide clarity on the regulatory environment for cryptocurrencies, and its passage could have a significant impact on the industry. Wit...

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 17
item.description: $383 million in dormant Bitcoin just moved for the first time in over eight years, sparking a flurry of speculation about the potential impact on the market. The wallet in question, which has been holding nearly 5,908 BTC since 2017, transferred its entire balance to a new address, leaving many to wonder what prompted this sudden activity. This massive movement of coins has significant implications for the Bitcoin ecosystem, as it could potentially disrupt the market's delicate balance and influ...

item.title: Citadel backs two rival crypto exchanges with $600 million — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 18
item.description: Citadel just invested a whopping $600 million in two rival crypto exchanges, Crypto.com and Kraken, as they both aggressively pursue the same tokenized-market expansion, with only one of them having disclosed Citadel's operational role so far. This is a bold move, especially considering the two exchanges are essentially competing for the same prize: a significant foothold in the Wall Street market. The fact that Citadel is backing both of them with such a massive investment suggests that they be...

item.title: Crypto executives say digital native generations may never — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 19
item.description: Eighty-four percent of financial firms now consider tokenization a strategic priority, which is a staggering shift in just a few years. This drastic change in attitude indicates that traditional financial institutions are finally taking notice of the potential of blockchain technology and its ability to transform the way we think about ownership and value. Tokenization, which is the process of converting traditional assets into digital tokens, has been gaining traction in recent years, and it se...

item.title: South Korea eyes September launch for second phase of CBDC — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 20
item.description: South Korea is gearing up to launch the second phase of its central bank digital currency pilot as early as September, with two regional banks set to join the program and new payment features to be tested. This is a significant development, as it marks a major milestone in the country's efforts to explore the potential of CBDCs. The Bank of Korea's pilot will also include tests of government subsidy payments using tokenized bank deposits, which could have far-reaching implications for the way ci...

item.title: Bitcoin ETFs Are Green Again—Here’s Why Investors Should — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 21
item.description: Two consecutive weeks of inflows have just ended the worst sustained outflow streak in Bitcoin ETF history, a brutal 17-week dry spell that had many investors on edge. This sudden turnaround is a welcome relief for those who have been riding out the market volatility, and it's a great reminder that patience can be a powerful strategy in the crypto space. After all, Bitcoin ETFs have been a wild ride lately, with many investors pulling out in droves as prices plummeted. But now that the tide is t...

item.title: Coinbase Wants To Be Canada’s ‘Everything Exchange — Crypto Daily Brief | Jul 22
item.description: Coinbase is working to bring Canadian customers tokenized stocks and prediction markets in a push to become the country's go-to "everything exchange", a move that could significantly blur the lines between traditional finance and crypto. This is a bold play by Coinbase, as it would allow users to trade a wide range of assets, from cryptocurrencies to stocks and even make predictions on future events, all in one place. The implications are huge, as it could make Coinbase a one-stop shop for Canad...
